commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=279518ba9ac955e6101e5c6abf4403c333655115)
commands.review_summary:
The PR defers live parent-menu rebuilds while provider data refresh is in flight, skips unused Grok local session scanning, caches Grok CLI version detection by binary path and mtime, and adds an investigation note.

Reproducibility: no. I did not establish a high-confidence local runtime reproduction in this read-only review. Source inspection confirms current main can repopulate an already-open menu from the scheduled rebuild path, and the PR body provides a detailed spindump-based reproduction narrative.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-provided real behavior proof is still needed, and maintainers should decide whether the focused test gap must be filled before merge.

target.title: fix: stop menu beachball when Grok provider is enabled
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes an app-only beachball (spinning rainbow cursor, ~1–12 s) that occurs on **every** menu-bar
open when the **Grok** provider is enabled. Disabling Grok avoids it entirely.

The freeze is not background-fetch workload and not subprocess spawn (both ruled out empirically). It
is a **WindowServer render bottleneck on CodexBar's own menu layer tree**, triggered by re-rendering
the *already-open* menu while Grok's slow fetch completes.

## Root cause (captured with `sudo spindump` during a held beachball)

- The app main thread is parked in `popUpStatusBarMenu → presentPopup:…animated: → nextEventMatchingMask
  → mach_msg`, ~0 CPU, "last ran 7 s ago" — it is waiting, not computing.
- The app's `NSEventThread` is **"blocked by turnstile waiting for WindowServer"**.
- **WindowServer's main thread burns ~3.2 s CPU** in a deeply recursive
  `CA::OGL::render_layers → CA::OGL::ImagingNode::render`, re-compositing CodexBar's menu layer tree.
- App-only because only CodexBar's tree is this expensive; WindowServer serves other apps fine in
  between.

The app's own minor stacks show the open menu being rebuilt **while tracked**:
`scheduleOpenMenuRebuildIfStillVisible → rebuildOpenMenuIfStillVisible → populateMenu`. Grok's fetch is
the slowest provider fetch (JSON-RPC `grok agent stdio` + web-billing fallback), so it completes
*after* the menu is already open and triggers a live `populateMenu` rebuild on every store tick,
re-submitting the menu's SwiftUI layer tree to WindowServer each time. Faster providers finish before
the menu opens, so Grok-off → no late rebuild → no freeze.

## Fix

In `StatusItemController.rebuildOpenMenuIfStillVisible(_:provider:)`: while a provider fetch is in
flight (`isMenuDataRefreshInFlight`), keep the content the open **parent** menu was populated with and
repopulate on the next open, instead of re-rendering live on every store tick. This stops the repeated
WindowServer re-composite that causes the beachball, and matches the intended "don't refresh while the
user is interacting; refresh on next open" model.

Exemptions (still rebuild immediately):
- User-driven **provider-switcher** rebuilds (detected via `openMenuRebuildsClosingHostedSubviewMenus`).
- Hosted-subview chart submenus.

## Secondary cleanup (orthogonal — did NOT fix the freeze, included as honest waste removal)

In the Grok provider path:
- Cache `GrokStatusProbe.detectVersion` by binary path + mtime instead of spawning the ~107 MB `grok`
  CLI (`grok --version`) on every fetch; the cache auto-invalidates when the binary is upgraded.
- Drop the per-fetch `GrokLocalSessionScanner.summarize` walk of `~/.grok/sessions/**/signals.json`
  (thousands of files); its result `localSummary` is never projected into any visible RateWindow or
  identity field.

## Testing

- `swift build` green.
- Beachball **no longer reproduces** with Grok enabled (manually verified on a packaged build).
- No new test regressions: the failing `StatusMenu*` / `MenuCardModel` tests on this base
  (`main`) fail identically without this change (verified by reverting only the menu edit); the
  `MenuCardModel` one is a `ko-KR` date-locale assertion unrelated to this change.

## Notes

A full investigation write-up (evidence, ruled-out hypotheses, capture protocol, and the alternative
"cheapen the Grok card" path if the inherent first-open render ever proves costly) is included at
`docs/perf-grok-menu-freeze-investigation-2026-06-10.md`.
target.author: jangisaac-dev
